name: Comillas Coastal Route
name_original: Comillas – circular a la Punta de la Moira
description: Hike the easy 2.7-mile Comillas – circular to Punta de la Moira trail for scenic coastal views and a historic viewpoint.
region_intro: Hiking around Ruiloba offers diverse trails along a dramatic coastline and through varied landscapes. The region is characterized by its rugged cliffs overlooking the Cantabrian Sea, providing expansive views and natural viewpoints. Inland, paths lead through rural areas, connecting natural beauty with cultural attractions. This area presents a range of hiking opportunities for different fitness levels. Best hiking trails around Ruiloba The most popular hiking route is Comillas Coastal Route, an easy 2.7 miles (4.3 km) trail that takes 1 hour 9 minutes to complete. This route offers scenic coastal views and passes a historic viewpoint. Another top favourite among local hikers is Mirador de la Corneja – View of the Cantabrian Sea loop from Ruiloba, a moderate 5.4 miles (8.7 km) path. This loop provides panoramic vistas of the Cantabrian Sea from a key natural viewpoint. Local hikers also love the Historic center of Comillas – El Capricho de Gaudí loop from Ruiloba, an 18.6 miles (29.9 km) trail leading through coastal areas and the historic center of Comillas, often completed in about 8 hours 6 minutes. Hiking around Ruiloba is defined by dramatic coastal cliffs, expansive views of the Cantabrian Sea, and paths connecting natural beauty with nearby towns. The network offers options for easy strolls, moderate loops, and more challenging routes with significant elevation gain. The routes in Ruiloba are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 200 reviews. More than 2000 hikers have used komoot to explore Ruiloba's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Ruiloba?
A: Ruiloba offers a diverse network of over 15 hiking trails, catering to various fitness levels. These routes explore the dramatic coastline, rural landscapes, and connect to nearby cultural attractions.

Q: Are there easy hiking trails suitable for beginners in Ruiloba?
A: Yes, Ruiloba has several easy hiking options perfect for beginners or those looking for a relaxed stroll. An excellent choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e998487707/comillas-coastal-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Comillas Coastal Route&lt;/a&gt;, an easy 2.7-mile (4.3 km) circular trail offering scenic coastal views and passing a historic viewpoint.

Q: What is the typical length and difficulty of hikes in Ruiloba?
A: Hikes in Ruiloba vary significantly in length and difficulty. You can find easy routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e998487707/comillas-coastal-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Comillas Coastal Route&lt;/a&gt; at just over 4 km, moderate loops such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20502858" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador de la Corneja – View of the Cantabrian Sea loop from Ruiloba&lt;/a&gt; which is about 8.7 km, and more challenging, longer trails like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10507521" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Iglesia de Ruiloba – Mirador de la Corneja loop from Ruiloba&lt;/a&gt;, which can extend to over 26 km with significant elevation gain.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see while hiking in Ruiloba?
A: Ruiloba's hiking trails are renowned for their breathtaking coastal landscapes, featuring dramatic cliffs and expansive views of the Cantabrian Sea. Inland, paths lead through charming rural areas, connecting natural beauty with cultural attractions. Key natural viewpoints like the Mirador de la Corneja offer spectacular panoramic vistas.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Ruiloba?
A: Yes, many of Ruiloba's trails are designed as circular routes, allowing you to start and end at the same point. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e998487707/comillas-coastal-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Comillas Coastal Route&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20502858" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador de la Corneja – View of the Cantabrian Sea loop from Ruiloba&lt;/a&gt;, both offering convenient loop options.

Q: What nearby attractions can I explore while hiking in Ruiloba?
A: Hiking in Ruiloba often provides opportunities to explore nearby cultural and natural attractions. You can visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/344751" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Historic center of Comillas&lt;/a&gt;, known for its architecture including Gaudí's El Capricho, or discover natural highlights like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5406017" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Oyambre Natural Park&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2589035" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Bolao Cliff&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Ruiloba?
A: The komoot community highly rates hiking in Ruiloba, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 270 reviews. Hikers often praise the stunning coastal views, the variety of trails for different skill levels, and the opportunity to combine natural exploration with visits to charming nearby towns.

Q: Is public transport available to reach hiking trails in Ruiloba?
A: While specific public transport links directly to trailheads might be limited, Ruiloba's proximity to towns like Comillas and Santillana del Mar suggests that local bus services could provide access to the general area, from which many trails are accessible. It's advisable to check local transport schedules for the most current information.

Q: Are there family-friendly hiking options in Ruiloba?
A: Yes, Ruiloba offers several family-friendly hiking options. Easy, shorter trails with minimal elevation gain, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e998487707/comillas-coastal-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Comillas Coastal Route&lt;/a&gt;, are ideal for families with children, providing beautiful scenery without being overly strenuous.

Q: Can I bring my dog on hiking trails in Ruiloba?
A: Generally, dogs are welcome on many hiking trails in Ruiloba, especially in natural outdoor areas. However, it's always recommended to keep them on a leash, especially in protected areas or when encountering wildlife or other hikers. Please be mindful of local regulations and signage.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Ruiloba?
A: The best time to hike in Ruiloba is typically during the spring (April to June) and autumn (September to October) when the weather is mild and pleasant, and the landscapes are vibrant. Summer (July and August) is also popular, but trails can be busier, and temperatures higher, especially along exposed coastal paths.

Q: Are there any challenging hikes for experienced hikers in Ruiloba?
A: Absolutely. For experienced hikers seeking a challenge, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38196742" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Historic center of Comillas – El Capricho de Gaudí loop from Ruiloba&lt;/a&gt; offer significant distance (nearly 30 km) and elevation gain, combining natural beauty with cultural exploration. The longer version of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10507521" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Iglesia de Ruiloba – Mirador de la Corneja loop from Ruiloba&lt;/a&gt; also presents a demanding option.
tips: La Punta de la Moira is a characteristic and recognized site in the town of Comillas. Historically it was where the fishermen arrived, today a viewpoint par excellence.

The place is traditionally known as the “City of Archbishops”. Fishing and agriculture have long been important, but more recently the primary sector has been displaced by the tertiary sector, i.e. tourism and services. King Alfonso XII and many nobles from Madrid, Barcelona, Seville and Bilbao preferred to spend part of their time in places like Comillas.

The municipality was the first settlement in Spain with electrically powered street lighting.


Comillas is rich in architecture of late historicism and early modernism, mainly due to the patronage of Claudio López Bru.

Some of the most outstanding examples:

Palacio de Sobrellano (built 1878-1890 by Joan Martorell for the Márques de Comillas)
Panteón de Sobranello (chapel right next to the Palacio de Sobranello, also built by Martorell in 1881, the furnishings come from young Antoni Gaudí)
Building of the Pontifical University of Comillas (Spanish: Universidad Pontificia Comillas), started in 1883 by Martorell, completed by Lluís Domènech i Montaner; The university was founded by Claudio López Bru in Comillas, the main campus is now in Madrid.

Source: Wikipedia
The Fuente-Farola Monument, a tribute from the town of Comillas to Joaquín de Pièlago, who finances the bringing of water to the town.

One of the many masterpieces of the famous Gaudí, the house called "Capricho" is perhaps not as recognized as his main works of art but it is undoubtedly a house to visit for its charm and characteristic architecture that make it a unique piece in its style. .

Beautiful hike on the Camino del Norte. The paths are each marked with yellow arrows or on the ground with the typical Camino shell. There are various hostels for pilgrims or possibly hotels. I chose Hotel. Overnight in Comillas at Hotel Solatorre. Hotel recommended, right on the Way of St. James.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trail?
faqs.answer: The trail typically starts in the town of Comillas. You can usually find parking within the town, which serves as a convenient starting point for this circular route.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for beginners or families with children?
faqs.answer: Yes, this trail is classified as an easy hike, making it suitable for all fitness levels, including beginners and families with children. It features minimal elevation gain and well-maintained paths.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this circular trail?
faqs.answer: The route is approximately 4.33 kilometers (about 2.7 miles) long and typically takes around 1 hour and 9 minutes to complete at a moderate pace.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the trail?
faqs.answer: You can expect easily accessible paths, including a dirt road that passes behind an old cemetery and continues along the coastline. The terrain is generally low technical difficulty, interspersed with small meadows and pine forests.

faqs.question: What are the main sights and viewpoints along the route?
faqs.answer: The route offers impressive views of the Cantabrian Sea. Key highlights include the Punta de la Moira Viewpoint, known as a "mirador por excelencia" (excellent viewpoint), and views of Playa de Fuentes. You'll also pass by the Comillas Beach.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this route?
faqs.answer: The trail can be enjoyed year-round, but spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and beautiful scenery. Summer is also popular, though it can be warmer. Always check the local weather forecast before heading out.

faqs.question: Is the trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Generally, trails in this area are dog-friendly, but it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially when passing through populated areas or near wildlife, and to clean up after them.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay an entrance fee for this hike?
faqs.answer: No, you do not need any special permits or have to pay an entrance fee to hike this trail. It is freely accessible to the public.

faqs.question: Does this trail connect with any other notable paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route also passes through sections of the Northern Route (Cantabria) and the Paseo de Jesús Cancio.

faqs.question: What should I bring for this hike?
faqs.answer: Given its easy nature, comfortable walking shoes, water, and sun protection (hat, sunscreen) are usually sufficient. A camera is recommended for the scenic coastal views.

faqs.question: Can I reach the trailhead using public transport?
faqs.answer: Comillas is a well-known town in Cantabria, and it's generally accessible by public transport, such as buses, from larger nearby cities. Check local bus schedules for routes to Comillas.
